fact: The first official Test cricket match was played between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground in 1877.
India won the ICC Men's Cricket World Cup in 1983 under captain Kapil Dev.
Sachin Tendulkar is the first cricketer to score 100 international centuries.
The first FIFA World Cup was held in Uruguay in 1930, and Uruguay won the tournament.
The UEFA Champions League is Europe's premier club football competition.
Lionel Messi won the FIFA World Cup with Argentina in 2022.
India won its first Thomas Cup title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia.
P. V. Sindhu won India's first Olympic silver medal in badminton at the 2016 Rio Olympics.
The shuttlecock can travel at speeds exceeding 400 km/h in professional badminton.
There are four Grand Slam tournaments: Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, and US Open.
Wimbledon is the oldest tennis tournament in the world.
Novak Djokovic has won multiple Grand Slam titles across all four majors.
The NBA was founded in 1946 as the Basketball Association of America.
Michael Jordan won six NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls.
A standard basketball team has five players on the court at a time.
